indication: Locally advanced (T4b any N or any T N2-3) or metastatic (any T any N M1) transitional cell carcinoma of the urothelial tract previously treated with platinum-based chemotherapy, OR first line treatment of locally advanced or metastatic urothelial cancer in patients ineligible for cisplatin-based chemotherapy. WHO performance status 0, 1, 2.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Administer using a low protein binding 0.2 micron filter. Maximum treatment duration two years (18 cycles). Treatment breaks up to 12 weeks beyond expected cycle length permitted only for immune toxicities.

pre_medications.indication: Infusion-related reaction management; maximum 4g/24h, 4 hours between doses

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold pembrolizumab and administer corticosteroids. Resume when liver function at NCI-CTC grade 1 following corticosteroid taper over at least 1 month.

dose_modifications.condition: AST/ALT ≥5xULN, or bilirubin >3xULN, or NCI-CTC grade 3 or above hepatic reaction, or no improvement to grade 1 within 12 weeks, or unable to taper corticosteroid to ≤10mg prednisolone/day within 12 weeks

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold and administer corticosteroids. Resume when reaction ≤grade 1 following corticosteroid taper over at least 1 month.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above nephritis, or no improvement to grade 1 within 12 weeks, or unable to taper corticosteroid to ≤10mg prednisolone/day within 12 weeks

dose_modifications.notes: Little need to adjust dose for haematological toxicity. Consider transfusion or erythropoietin if Hb <8g/dL or symptomatic anaemia.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 pneumonitis, nephritis, infusion-related reaction, or NCI-CTC grade 4 adverse reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue pembrolizumab. Dose reductions are NOT permitted for pembrolizumab.
Withhold and administer corticosteroids. Resume at grade 1 following 1-month taper. Permanently discontinue if no improvement to grade 1 within 12 weeks.
Withhold pembrolizumab. Corticosteroids 1–2mg/kg/day prednisone equivalent. Resume if improves to grade 0–1 within 12 weeks and steroids tapered to ≤10mg prednisolone. Discontinue on second episode.
Current funding approval precludes further treatment after an interruption of 12 weeks or longer.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: No specific haematological thresholds for treatment delay stated. FBC, LFTs and U&Es required prior to day 1 of each cycle. Thyroid function tests prior to starting and before each cycle.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 of each cycle
Thyroid function tests prior to starting treatment and before each cycle (or when clinically indicated)
Monitor for immune-related adverse reactions: pneumonitis, nephritis, colitis, hepatitis, endocrinopathies, skin reactions, ocular reactions
special_warnings: Pembrolizumab is an immunotherapy; dose reductions are NOT permitted – only delays or permanent discontinuation
Avoid systemic corticosteroids before starting pembrolizumab; they may be used after initiation for immune-related adverse reactions
Autoimmune toxicities can be severe or life-threatening; early diagnosis and management essential
Immune-related adverse reactions may occur during treatment or months after the last dose
Maximum treatment duration is two years (18 cycles of 42 days)
Formal medical review required at least by end of cycle 3
Treatment breaks up to 12 weeks beyond expected cycle length allowed only for immune toxicities to settle
No prior anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L1, anti-PD-L2, anti-CD137, or anti-CTLA-4 antibody treatment (unless under pembrolizumab compassionate use programme)
Pembrolizumab must be given as monotherapy

required_patient_fields.brain_metastases_status: No symptomatically active brain or leptomeningeal metastases

drugs.special_instructions: Administer using a low protein binding 0.2 micron filter. Maximum treatment duration two years (18 cycles). Treatment breaks up to 12 weeks beyond expected cycle length permitted only for immune toxicities.
drugs.notes: Once every 42 days | Administer using a low protein binding 0.2 micron filter. Maximum treatment duration two years (18 cycles). Treatment breaks up to 12 weeks beyond expected cycle length permitted only for immune toxicities.

indication: First line treatment of advanced or metastatic renal cell carcinoma. WHO Performance status 0, 1. Palliative intent.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Once daily for 28 days followed by 14-day rest period (42-day cycle)
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Available as 12.5mg, 25mg, 37.5mg and 50mg capsules. Dose modifications in 12.5mg steps; minimum dose 25mg daily. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert applies. Must only be prescribed under oncologist supervision.

take_home_medicines.instructions: 50mg once daily for 28 days followed by 14-day rest period
4mg after first loose stool then 2–4mg four times a day as required for diarrhoea (maximum 16mg/24 hours)

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery to ≥1x10⁹/L. If recovery within 7 days continue at last dose. If recovery takes longer than 7 days reduce dose by 12.5mg.
Delay until recovery to ≥75x10⁹/L. If recovery within 7 days continue at full dose. If recovery takes longer than 7 days reduce dose by 12.5mg.
50mg daily (no dose reduction required for Child-Pugh A or B). No information for Child-Pugh C. No information for AST/ALT >2.5xULN (or >5xULN with liver metastases).

dose_modifications.condition: Persistent BP >140/90mmHg despite standard antihypertensive therapy
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose in 12.5mg steps and continue monitoring. If hypertension persists discontinue sunitinib.
Stop until resolves to at least grade 2, then restart with 12.5mg dose reduction.
Delay until resolves to grade 1. Restart with 12.5mg dose reduction.
Delay until resolves to grade 1. Restart with 12.5–25mg dose reduction.
Treatment should be stopped if a third dose reduction is necessary.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Prior to cycle 1: neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L, platelets ≥75x10⁹/L. FBCs, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle for first three cycles (then every other cycle if stable). Minimum sunitinib dose 25mg daily; dose modifications in 12.5mg steps.

monitoring_requirements: FBCs, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle for first three cycles; may reduce to every other cycle if stable
Blood pressure weekly for first 4 weeks then every 6–12 weeks
Thyroid function tests at baseline then every 3 months
Baseline LVEF in patients with cardiac history or elderly; repeat every 3–6 months as clinically indicated
special_warnings: Dose modifications in 12.5mg steps; minimum dose 25mg daily
All dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval
If third dose reduction required, stop treatment
Hypothyroidism can occur – manage per standard practice, no need to discontinue or dose reduce
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert must be followed
Must only be prescribed under oncologist supervision
8 cycles will be set in ARIA (until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity)

required_patient_fields.cardiac_history: Baseline LVEF if cardiac history or elderly

drugs.frequency: Once daily for 28 days followed by 14-day rest period (42-day cycle)
drugs.special_instructions: Available as 12.5mg, 25mg, 37.5mg and 50mg capsules. Dose modifications in 12.5mg steps; minimum dose 25mg daily. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert applies. Must only be prescribed under oncologist supervision.
drugs.notes: Once daily for 28 days followed by 14-day rest period (42-day cycle) | Available as 12.5mg, 25mg, 37.5mg and 50mg capsules. Dose modifications in 12.5mg steps; minimum dose 25mg daily. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert applies. Must only be prescribed under oncologist supervision.

indication: First line treatment of advanced renal cell cancer with poor prognostic features. Performance status 0, 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose rounded to nearest 5mg. May reduce infusion to 30 minutes if well tolerated. Must be administered in a non-PVC bag via a low-adsorption administration set with an in-line filter of 0.2–5 microns. 24 cycles set in ARIA.

take_home_medicines.instructions: Three times a day for 3 days then as required

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until counts recover. If recovery takes 8 days or longer, reduce dose by 5mg/week until tolerated.
Delay until counts recover. If recovery takes 8 days or longer, reduce dose by 5mg/week until tolerated.

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin <3xULN and any AST/ALT and Child-Pugh A or B
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment needed. Applies to patients with baseline platelet count ≥100x10⁹/L.

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin >3xULN and any AST/ALT or Child-Pugh C
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 10mg weekly. Applies to patients with baseline platelet count ≥100x10⁹/L.
No dose adjustment required. However, temsirolimus can induce renal failure; monitor closely and discontinue if renal failure occurs.
Does not require discontinuation. Consider 5mg dose reduction.
Interrupt treatment and consider short-term corticosteroids. Resume with 5mg dose reduction once resolved.
Treat appropriately and permanently discontinue temsirolimus.
Treatment should be stopped if a third dose reduction is necessary.

dose_modifications.condition: Hypersensitivity or anaphylaxis during administration despite pre-medication
dose_modifications.notes: Stop infusion immediately, observe for ≥30 minutes. At medical discretion may resume after additional chlorphenamine and/or ranitidine 50mg IV given 30 minutes before restarting at half the infusion rate.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Weekly U&Es, LFTs, FBC and blood glucose for first 6 weeks; may reduce to fortnightly if stable. Serum cholesterol and triglycerides at baseline then every 12 weeks. Dose rounded to nearest 5mg.

monitoring_requirements: Weekly U&Es, LFTs, FBC and blood glucose for first 6 weeks; then fortnightly if results and patient stable
Serum cholesterol and triglycerides prior to starting therapy and every 12 weeks thereafter
Monitor for interstitial lung disease
Monitor renal function closely (temsirolimus can induce renal failure)
special_warnings: This protocol may require funding approval
Temsirolimus must be administered in a non-PVC bag via a low-adsorption set with 0.2–5 micron in-line filter
Dose rounded to nearest 5mg
Infusion may be reduced to 30 minutes if well tolerated
All dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval
Third dose reduction = stop treatment
Temsirolimus can induce renal failure; discontinue if renal failure occurs
24 cycles set in ARIA (until unacceptable toxicity or disease progression)

required_patient_fields.baseline_lipids: Serum cholesterol and triglycerides required at baseline

drugs.special_instructions: Dose rounded to nearest 5mg. May reduce infusion to 30 minutes if well tolerated. Must be administered in a non-PVC bag via a low-adsorption administration set with an in-line filter of 0.2–5 microns. 24 cycles set in ARIA.
drugs.notes: Once weekly (7-day cycle) | Dose rounded to nearest 5mg. May reduce infusion to 30 minutes if well tolerated. Must be administered in a non-PVC bag via a low-adsorption administration set with an in-line filter of 0.2–5 microns. 24 cycles set in ARIA.

indication: Confirmed histological diagnosis of renal cell carcinoma with a clear cell component that is locally advanced or metastatic; treatment naïve to systemic therapy (no prior VEGF-targeted or mTOR inhibitor-targeted therapy, unless prior pazopanib or sunitinib stopped solely due to dose-limiting toxicity in clear absence of progression). WHO Performance status 0, 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Once daily for 21 days followed by 7-day rest period (28-day cycle)
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Available as 1340 microgram and 890 microgram capsules. May be taken with or without food. If a dose is missed do not give an additional dose; take usual dose next day. Reduced dose level: 890 microgram once daily on same 21/7 schedule. NPSA/2008/RRR001 applies. 12 cycles set in ARIA.

take_home_medicines.instructions: 1340 microgram once daily for 21 days followed by 7-day rest period
4mg after first loose stool then 2–4mg four times a day as required for diarrhoea (maximum 16mg/24 hours)

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery to ≥1x10⁹/L. If recovery within 7 days continue at last dose.
Delay until recovery to ≥100x10⁹/L. If recovery within 7 days continue at last dose.
Use with caution with close monitoring; no initial dose adjustment unless in response to toxicity.
Prescribe 1340 microgram once daily on alternate days due to increased exposure risk.
Tivozanib is not recommended in severe hepatic impairment.
Caution advised due to limited experience.

dose_modifications.condition: Severe hypertension not controlled with medical management
dose_modifications.notes: Temporary suspension recommended. Resume once controlled. In persistent hypertension, reduce dose or interrupt and re-initiate at lower dose. Discontinue for persistent severe hypertension, PRES, or other complications.
Reduce dose. First reduction level: 890 microgram once daily.
Treatment should be stopped if a third dose reduction is necessary.
Discontinue tivozanib prior to major surgery. Resume only after assessment of wound healing.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Prior to cycle 1: neutrophils >1x10⁹/L, platelets >100x10⁹/L. FBCs, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle. No treatment breaks >6 weeks beyond expected cycle length. Formal review at least by end of first 8 weeks. There is a correlation between overall survival and cumulative dose exposure; manage toxicity before dose reduction.

monitoring_requirements: FBCs, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle
Blood pressure weekly for first 4 weeks then every 4–12 weeks
Thyroid function tests at baseline then every 12 weeks
Baseline LVEF in patients with cardiac history or elderly; repeat every 12–24 weeks as clinically indicated
Proteinuria monitoring before starting treatment and every 12 weeks (urine dipstick); 24-hour urine protein if 2+ on dipstick
Baseline and periodic ECG monitoring; maintain electrolytes (calcium, magnesium, potassium) within normal range (QT prolongation risk)
special_warnings: Correlation between overall survival and cumulative dose exposure; manage toxicity before reducing dose
Avoid verapamil and diltiazem (CYP3A4 inhibitors) during tivozanib treatment
Tivozanib may interact with many other agents – always check for drug interactions
Dose reduction level: 890 microgram once daily (same 21/7 schedule)
No treatment breaks >6 weeks beyond expected cycle length
Prior to major surgery, discontinue tivozanib; resume only after wound healing assessment
QT/QTc interval prolongation reported – use with caution in patients with QT history
All dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval
Third dose reduction = stop treatment
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert applies; must only be prescribed under oncologist supervision
12 cycles set in ARIA (until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity)

required_patient_fields.prior_systemic_therapy: Treatment naïve (no prior VEGF-targeted or mTOR inhibitor therapy)

drugs.frequency: Once daily for 21 days followed by 7-day rest period (28-day cycle)
drugs.special_instructions: Available as 1340 microgram and 890 microgram capsules. May be taken with or without food. If a dose is missed do not give an additional dose; take usual dose next day. Reduced dose level: 890 microgram once daily on same 21/7 schedule. NPSA/2008/RRR001 applies. 12 cycles set in ARIA.
drugs.notes: Once daily for 21 days followed by 7-day rest period (28-day cycle) | Available as 1340 microgram and 890 microgram capsules. May be taken with or without food. If a dose is missed do not give an additional dose; take usual dose next day. Reduced dose level: 890 microgram once daily on same 21/7 schedule. NPSA/2008/RRR001 applies. 12 cycles set in ARIA.

indication: Bladder cancer that has failed to respond or progressed within twelve months of receiving a platinum based therapy.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Starting dose 320mg/m² for PS 0 with no prior pelvic irradiation. Starting dose 280mg/m² for PS 1 or PS 0 with prior pelvic irradiation; increase to 320mg/m² from cycle 2 if no haematological toxicity causing delay or reduction in cycle 1. Dose banded per national dose bands. Patients ≥75 to <80 years: 280mg/m². Patients ≥80 years: 250mg/m². Vesicant – extravasation risk. NPSA/2008/RRR04 vinca alkaloid IV safety alert applies. Advise patients to drink ≥1500ml/day for 5 days post-treatment.

take_home_medicines.instructions: Three times a day as required (cycle 1 only; further supplies from supportive therapies folder)
Twice a day for 7 days then as required (to prevent and treat constipation)
At night for 7 days then as required (to prevent and treat constipation)

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 neutropenia OR febrile neutropenia: first event at starting dose 320mg/m²
Grade 4 neutropenia OR febrile neutropenia: second consecutive event at starting dose 320mg/m²
Grade 4 neutropenia OR febrile neutropenia: third consecutive event at starting dose 320mg/m²
Grade 4 neutropenia OR febrile neutropenia: first event at starting dose 280mg/m²
Grade 4 neutropenia OR febrile neutropenia: second consecutive event at starting dose 280mg/m²
Mucositis or constipation grade 2 for >5 days or grade 3 for any duration: first event at starting dose 320mg/m²
Mucositis or constipation grade 2 for >5 days or grade 3 for any duration: second consecutive event at starting dose 320mg/m²
Mucositis or constipation grade 2 for >5 days or grade 3 for any duration: third consecutive event at starting dose 320mg/m²
Mucositis or constipation grade 2 for >5 days or grade 3 for any duration: first event at starting dose 280mg/m²
Mucositis or constipation grade 2 for >5 days or grade 3 for any duration: second consecutive event at starting dose 280mg/m²

dose_modifications.parameter: Any other toxicity grade 3 or above (except nausea and vomiting)
Any other toxicity grade 3 or above (except nausea and vomiting)
dose_modifications.condition: Second consecutive event at starting dose 320mg/m²

dose_modifications.parameter: Any other toxicity grade 3 or above (except nausea and vomiting)
dose_modifications.condition: Third consecutive event at starting dose 320mg/m²

dose_modifications.parameter: Any other toxicity grade 3 or above (except nausea and vomiting)
Any other toxicity grade 3 or above (except nausea and vomiting)
dose_modifications.condition: Second consecutive event at starting dose 280mg/m²
Child-Pugh A or prothrombin time >60% norm and bilirubin 1.5–3xULN with transaminases above ULN and/or GGT >5xULN
Child-Pugh B or prothrombin time >50% above norm and bilirubin >3xULN with transaminases and GGT above ULN
Child-Pugh C, prothrombin time <50%, bilirubin >5xULN, isolated transaminases >2.5xULN (>5xULN with liver metastases), or GGT >15xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Vinflunine not evaluated in severe hepatic impairment – do not use.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: FBC, LFTs, U&Es prior to each cycle. Starting dose depends on PS and prior pelvic irradiation history: 280mg/m² for PS 1 or PS 0 with prior pelvic irradiation (may escalate to 320mg/m² from cycle 2 if no haematological toxicity in cycle 1); 320mg/m² for PS 0 without prior pelvic irradiation. Dose banded per national dose bands.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs, U&Es prior to each cycle
Monitor for constipation – prescribe laxatives prophylactically
Monitor for peripheral neuropathy
special_warnings: Vinflunine is a VESICANT – follow extravasation procedures
NPSA/2008/RRR04 vinca alkaloid IV safety alert must be followed
Starting dose varies by PS and prior pelvic irradiation: 280mg/m² for PS 1 or PS 0 with prior pelvic irradiation; 320mg/m² for all others
Patients ≥75 to <80 years: dose 280mg/m²; patients ≥80 years: dose 250mg/m²
Check dose before administration (Warning in regimen summary)
Advise patients to drink ≥1500ml liquid per day for 5 days post-treatment
All dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval
Third dose reduction = discontinue treatment
Dose banded per national dose bands

required_patient_fields.age: Required to determine starting dose (≥75 and ≥80 year dose adjustments)
required_patient_fields.creatinine_clearance: Required to determine dose in renal impairment

indication: Stage I seminoma of the testis with no metastases (retroperitoneal lymph nodes ≤10mm and normal levels of AFP/HCG), post orchidectomy.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded per national dose bands (10mg/ml). AUC dosing: use EDTA or uncorrected Tc99 clearance to calculate GFR – Cockcroft & Gault formula is NOT recommended for this regimen. Irritant – extravasation risk.

take_home_medicines.instructions: Twice a day for 3 days starting day after chemotherapy (day 2). Take with or after food.
Three times a day for 3 days starting on day of chemotherapy, then 10mg three times a day as required for nausea. Supply 5 days or original pack.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Prior to treatment: neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L, platelets ≥100x10⁹/L. GFR must be measured by EDTA or uncorrected Tc99 clearance (not Cockcroft-Gault). Single cycle regimen only.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es
EDTA or uncorrected Tc99 GFR measurement (Cockcroft-Gault NOT recommended for this regimen)
special_warnings: Single cycle regimen – given once post orchidectomy
GFR MUST be measured by EDTA or uncorrected Tc99 clearance; Cockcroft-Gault formula is NOT recommended
Carboplatin is an IRRITANT – follow extravasation procedures
Dose banded per national dose bands (10mg/ml)
AUC 7 dosing – dose calculated using measured GFR (Calvert formula)
Verify cycle length against source NHS UHS PDF — assumed 21 days.

auc_dosing.gfr_method: EDTA or uncorrected Tc99 clearance (Cockcroft-Gault NOT recommended)
auc_dosing.notes: Carboplatin dose (mg) = AUC x (GFR + 25). GFR must be measured by EDTA or uncorrected Tc99 clearance.

required_patient_fields.gfr_measured: EDTA or uncorrected Tc99 clearance required pre-treatment

drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded per national dose bands (10mg/ml). AUC dosing: use EDTA or uncorrected Tc99 clearance to calculate GFR – Cockcroft & Gault formula is NOT recommended for this regimen. Irritant – extravasation risk.
drugs.notes: Single cycle (one dose post orchidectomy) | Dose banded per national dose bands (10mg/ml). AUC dosing: use EDTA or uncorrected Tc99 clearance to calculate GFR – Cockcroft & Gault formula is NOT recommended for this regimen. Irritant – extravasation risk.
